items.headline: US trade court rules against president's 10% global tariffs
items.body: A federal trade court has ruled the administration’s 10% global tariffs are unlawful. This decision forces a halt to collecting the duties, impacting billions in trade.

items.headline: Zambia rejects US health deal over data and minerals clauses
items.body: Zambia rejected a US health funding deal worth up to two billion dollars. The decision halts a new US aid model over data privacy and mineral rights fears.

items.headline: National strike escalates in Bolivia amid economic crisis
items.body: A national strike by workers over fuel prices and inflation has intensified. The growing unrest creates a major challenge for the government's stability.

items.headline: Insurgents kill dozens in coordinated attacks in central Mali
items.body: Al-Qaeda affiliated insurgents killed dozens of people in two village attacks. The coordinated assault signals a worsening security crisis for the ruling junta.

es.headline: Spanish food industry exports fall 8% this year
es.body: Spain's food industry exports have fallen over 8% in 2026 due to geopolitics. This shrinks international markets, pressuring a key sector of the national economy.

no.headline: Norway's central bank raises key policy interest rate to 4.25 percent
no.body: Norges Bank raised its main interest rate by 0.25 percentage points to fight inflation. The move increases borrowing costs for households and businesses across the country.

no.headline: Norway opens for testing of driverless vehicles on public roads
no.body: The Public Roads Administration now permits testing self-driving vehicles without a safety driver. This change advances the potential for commercial autonomous transport solutions in the country.

uk.headline: Labour government suffers significant losses in UK-wide elections
uk.body: The party lost control of key councils and seats in England, Scotland, and Wales. This result empowers opposition parties and signals a major shift in the political landscape.

nl.headline: EU to introduce stricter rules for heavy fireworks after Dutch appeal
nl.body: The European Commission is set to introduce stricter fireworks regulations. This follows an urgent appeal from the Netherlands over their use in attacks.

nl.headline: National tech accelerator splits to create dedicated deeptech support
nl.body: The Dutch tech accelerator Techleap has been restructured into two separate entities. The change creates focused public funding for strategic deeptech and AI startups.
